questions.question: Do I need a permit to remove a tree on my private property, how do I apply, and who reviews it?
questions.answer: Yes, you need a permit to remove a tree on your private property in New Castle if it falls under the regulated activities outlined in §121-3. This includes clearing, removal of trees within the regulated landscape buffer zone with a DBH of four inches or more, removal of trees outside this zone with a DBH of eight inches or more, removal of specimen trees, and removal of street trees. To apply, you must file an application with the approving authority, which is the Town Environmental Coordinator, unless the property is subject to a pending site plan, subdivision, or special permit application, in which case the Planning Board or Zoning Board is the approving authority. The application must include detailed information such as the property owner's details, a description of the proposed removal, and a tree replacement plan if applicable. The approving authority will review the application and make a determination based on an inspection and the details provided.

questions.question: What are the penalties for removing trees without a permit?
questions.answer: The penalties for removing trees without a permit in the Town of New Castle are as follows: a fine of $250 per tree for trees with a DBH of 4 inches to 12 inches, $750 per tree for trees with a DBH of 12 inches to 18 inches, $1,500 per tree for trees with a DBH of 18 inches to 24 inches, and $2,500 per tree for trees with a DBH of 24 inches or more. Each violation is considered a separate offense. Additionally, offenders may be ordered by the court to replant trees that were improperly removed. The fines collected are deposited into the Tree Bank Fund. Furthermore, the Town will not issue a building permit, temporary certificate of occupancy, or certificate of occupancy for any property with an unresolved violation of this chapter.

questions.question: Are there fees associated with tree removal permits or for removing individual trees?
questions.answer: Yes, there are fees associated with tree removal permits. An application fee and inspection fee are required, as set forth by resolution of the Town Board in the Master Fee Schedule. Additionally, if tree replacement requirements cannot be met on-site, a fee may be paid to the Tree Bank Fund as mitigation for the removal of trees.

questions.question: Can I replant trees to replace removed trees, and are they required to be of certain species?
questions.answer: Yes, you can replant trees to replace removed trees in New Castle, and there are specific requirements regarding the species. Tree replacement must occur on-site unless deemed impracticable or unwarranted by the approving authority, in which case a fee may be paid to the Tree Bank Fund. The total aggregate diameter of trees removed must be replaced with new trees according to specified percentages. Replanted trees should be ecologically compatible with the site and neighboring properties and should be of the same or similar species as those removed when practical. Invasive trees are not allowed, and deviations from the recommended species list require prior approval. At least 50% of replacement trees must be canopy trees, and 25% must be understory trees. All replacement trees must meet minimum size requirements.

questions.question: Which neighbors must be notified if I remove trees?
questions.answer: If you are removing three or more trees, you must notify all property owners of record within 100 feet of the boundaries of the property on which the proposed regulated activity will be conducted. This notification must be mailed by certified mail, return receipt requested, using a form provided by the Town.

questions.question: Does the code recognize the importance of keeping a healthy tree canopy?
questions.answer: Yes, the code recognizes the importance of keeping a healthy tree canopy. The legislative intent in §121-1 highlights the significance of trees in providing shade, enhancing air quality, reducing energy costs, and contributing to the aesthetic and environmental quality of the community. The code emphasizes the preservation of trees to maintain the existing character of the Town and enhance property values.

questions.question: What are my responsibilities for maintaining trees on my property?
questions.answer: As a property owner in the Town of New Castle, you are responsible for maintaining trees on your property by ensuring that any removal of trees complies with the town's regulations. This includes obtaining a tree removal permit for activities such as clearing, removing trees within regulated landscape buffer zones, removing specimen trees, or working within the critical root zone of street trees. You are also responsible for replacing trees that are removed, either on-site or by contributing to the Tree Bank Fund if on-site replacement is impracticable. Additionally, you must ensure that any tree removal does not negatively impact erosion, drainage, adjacent vegetation, property values, or wildlife habitat.

questions.question: Is data collected on the reasons for tree removals?
questions.answer: The statute does not specify that data is collected on the reasons for tree removals.
